title: hippocrepis squamata subsp. hieronymi
intro: Hippocrepis squamata subsp. hieronymi, also known as the hairy broom-spurge or broom-spurge, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hippocrepis squamata subsp. hieronymi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried root or leaf infusion, and it can also be used topically in the form of ointments or creams.
